title: Location Areas, Encounter Relations, Filtering, and Exploration · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed ranger review reconstructs location areas, encounter relations, filtering, and exploration from revision, command, arguments, request identity, response fixture, state transition, cache decision, goroutine ownership, output, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for provenance-preserving location encounter explorer after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging…
objectives: Distinguish region, location, and location-area resources and map the command to the endpoint whose relation answers the user need.
Validate numeric or canonical area identity, retain requested and resolved values, and construct only fixed-origin resource paths.
Deduplicate Pokemon by stable identity while retaining area, version, method, chance, or condition provenance needed by the view.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Test empty, duplicate, multi-version, malformed relation, large, filtered, cached, and changed-area fixtures.

hints: For Pokedex product engineering and Go Record what required effort · Review Pokedex Go Explore Fixtures, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Pokedex Go Explore Fixtures precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Pokedex product engineering and Go Record what required effort · Review Pokedex Go Explore Fixtures, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A review Go team handles case 7158 during a fair-use API stewardship review. Build deterministic Go evidence to admit bounded location-area encounters and preserve relation provenance through display; no user-visible meaning may rely on color or cursor motion; then inject one cache, waiter, goroutine, cancellation, or queue schedule and retain ownership evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Test empty, duplicate, multi-version, malformed relation, large, filtered, cached, and changed-area fixtures.

For Pokedex product engineering and Go Record what required effort · Review Pokedex Go Explore Fixtures, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A popular area with many encounters represents all explore cases.
